trans-unit.source: Specifies the URL of the Shopify Admin you are connecting to. Use the format: "https://{store ID}.myshopify.com". You can build the URL by combining the store ID from the admin URL, e.g., "admin.shopify.com/store/{store ID}" and ".myshopify.com". Simply copy the URL from the Shopify Admin, and the connector will convert it to the required format. Ensure you copy the URL from the Shopify Admin, not the online store, as the online store may display a redirect URL.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control ShopUrl - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control ShopUrl - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: This guide can help you quickly get started with Business Central. You can import the products and customers from Shopify that you want to manage in Business Central. You can use this guide only one time
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control6 - Property InstructionalText

trans-unit.source: Get started with Business Central by importing data from Shopify
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control6 - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies if customers are imported from Shopify.

Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Import Customers From Shopify - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies if products are imported from Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Import Products From Shopify - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Import Products From Shopify - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: You already have items in Business Central. Review the settings for your shop to ensure that your Shopify products map correctly to Business Central items, and then manually import your products.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control7 - Property InstructionalText

trans-unit.source: Sorry, we can't import your data.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control7 - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Products in Shopify are called items in Business Central. This guide can help you import your Shopify products to Business Central. You can use this guide only one time.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control8 - Property InstructionalText
Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control8 - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Here are the important settings for getting started.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control9 - Property InstructionalText

trans-unit.source: Specifies the item template to use when creating items in Business Central. These are products in Shopify that don't exist as items in Business Central.

trans-unit.source: You've chosen to import customers. Here are some important settings that define how to create them in Business Central.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control10 - Property InstructionalText
Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control10 - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: You should only import customers for whom you want to track order activity in Business Central. For example, the customers that you often do business with.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control11 - Property InstructionalText

trans-unit.source: Specifies the customer template to use to create unknown customers. These are customers in Shopify that don't already exist in Business Central.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Customer Template Code - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Customer Template Code - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Orders that you synch from Shopify can automatically become sales orders in Business Central (recommended). The item quantities on these orders are then part of inventory planning and availability in Business Central. You can also import orders to the Shopify Orders page and then turn them into sales orders manually.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Connector Guide - Control Control12 - Property InstructionalText

trans-unit.source: Specify how orders should be created

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether synchronization runs in the background. When enabled, you can continue working while large data sets synchronize. Disable for demos or troubleshooting to see real-time progress and receive detailed error messages.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AllowBackgroudSyncs - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the date on which Business Central will no longer support Shopify Admin API version. To continue to use your integration, upgrade to the latest version of Business Central before this date.

Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control API Version Expiry Date - Property Caption
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Code - Property AboutTitle

trans-unit.source: Specifies a code to identify this Shopify Shop.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Code - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Give your shop a name that will make it easy to find in Business Central. For example, a name might reflect what a shop sells, such as Furniture or Coffee, or the country or region it serves.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Code - Property AboutText

trans-unit.source: Specifies the currency of the Shopify Shop. Enter a currency code only if your online shop uses a different currency than the local currency (LCY). The specified currency must have exchange rates configured. If your online shop uses the same currency as Business Central, leave the field empty.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control CurrencyCode - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Enabled - Property AboutTitle

trans-unit.source: Specifies if the service is enabled.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Enabled - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: We just need the shop name and URL to connect it to Shopify. When you have checked all shop settings, enable the connection here.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Enabled - Property AboutText

trans-unit.source: Specifies if an API access token is available for this store. The token allows the connector to access your shop's data as long as the app is installed. To acquire a token, turn on the Enabled toggle or use the Request Access action.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control HasAccessKey - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control HasAccessKey - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies the language of the Shopify Shop.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control LanguageCode - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether the log is activated.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control LoggingMode - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the version of Shopify Admin API used by current version of the Shopify connector.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Shopify Admin API Version - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Shopify Admin API Version - Property Caption
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Shopify URL - Property AboutTitle

trans-unit.source: Specifies the URL of the Shopify Admin you are connecting to. Use the format: "https://{store ID}.myshopify.com". You can build the URL by combining the store ID from the admin URL, e.g., "admin.shopify.com/store/{store ID}" and ".myshopify.com". Simply copy the URL from the Shopify Admin, and the connector will convert it to the required format. Ensure you copy the URL from the Shopify Admin, not the online store, as the online store may display a redirect URL.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Shopify URL - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Provide the URL that people will use to access your shop. For example, *https://myshop.myshopify.com*.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Shopify URL - Property AboutText
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ItemSync - Property AboutTitle

trans-unit.source: **Products** in Shopify are called **Items** in Business Central. Define how to synchronize items in *this* shop with Business Central. If one of the apps doesn't have this data, you can quickly export items from Business Central to Shopify and vice versa.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ItemSync - Property AboutText
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ItemSync - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies if unknown items are automatically created in D365BC when synchronizing from Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoCreateUnknownItems - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether D365BC can update products when synchronizing to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control CanUpdateShopifyProducts - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the status of a product in Shopify when an item is create in Shopify via the sync.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control CreateProductStatusValue - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if you want to prevent negative inventory. With "continue" the inventory can go negative, with "Deny" you want to prevent negative inventory.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control DefaultInventoryPolicy - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if you want to manage your inventory in Shopify based on D365BC.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control InventoryTracket - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if only the items that are mapped to Shopify products/Shopify variants are synchronized from Posted Sales Invoices to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Items Mapped to Products - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies which item template to use when creating unknown items.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ItemTemplCode - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the variant option name for the unit of measure.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control OptionNameForUOM - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether product/variant metafields are synchronized to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Product Metafields To Shopify - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the status of a product in Shopify via the sync when an item is blocked or removed from the Shopify Product in Business Central.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control RemoveProductAction - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether Shopify can update items when synchronizing from Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ShopifyCanUpdateItems - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies a field separator for the SKU if you use "Item. No + Variant Code" to create a variant.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SKUFieldSeparator - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if and based on what you want to create variants in D365BC.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SKUType - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies in which direction items are synchronized.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncItem - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether you want to synchronize item attributes to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncItemAttributes - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether you want to synchronize extended texts to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncItemExtendedText - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether you want to synchronize item images and in which direction.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncItemImages - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether you want to synchronize marketing texts to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncItemMarketingText - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if you want to have the different unit of measures as an variant in Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control UOMAsVariant - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the prefix for variants. The variants you have defined in Shopify are created in Business Central based on an increasing number.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control VariantPrefix - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies the weight unit of the Shopify Shop.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control WeightUnit - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control OrderProcessing - Property AboutTitle

trans-unit.source: Define how new orders in Shopify flow into Business Central. For example, you can require that Shopify orders are approved before they become a sales order or invoice in Business Central. You can also define how to post shipping revenue, and the address that determines where you pay taxes.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control OrderProcessing - Property AboutText
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control OrderProcessing - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether Shopify orders are automatically archived when they are paid, fulfilled, and have associated sales documents with all lines shipped.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control ArchiveProcessOrders - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether orders may be created automatically.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoCreateOrders - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoCreateOrders - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies if a Sales Order should be releases after creation
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoReleaseSalesOrders - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoReleaseSalesOrders - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether to automatically synchronize orders when they’re created in Shopify. Shopify will notify Business Central that orders are ready. Business Central will schedule the Sync Orders from Shopify job on the Job Queue Entries page. The user account of the person who turns on this toggle will be used to run the job. That user must have permission to create background tasks in the job queue.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoSyncOrders - Property ToolTip
Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control AutoSyncOrders - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies the general ledger account to use when you post cash rounding differences from Shopify POS transactions.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control CashRoundingsAccount - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies which currency is used in Shopify orders processing. Using presentment currency may cause differences between amounts in LCY after posting documents.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Currency Handling - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies if Business Central document no. is synchronized to Shopify as order attribute.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Order Attributes To Shopify - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether the posted sales invoices can be synchronized to Shopify.
trans-unit.note: Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control Posted Invoice Sync - Property ToolTip

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether the customer is notified when the shipment is synchronized to Shopify.

trans-unit.source: Specifies the G/L Account for posting the shipping cost.

trans-unit.source: Specifies whether the Shopify Order No. is shown in the document line.

trans-unit.source: Specifies the G/L Account for to post the sold gift card amounts.

Page Shpfy Shop Card - Control SyncOrderJobQueueUser - Property Caption

trans-unit.source: Specifies the tax area source and the sequence to be followed.

trans-unit.source: Set up default customer accounts or templates per country or regions. The designated default customer account for a specific country or region will take precedence over the value in the Shopify Shop card page. Additionally, you may specify tax settings by county or province to ensure more accurate tax calculations.
